solutions.title: Traceability is now achievable, All the way.
description: Edraak Systems offers complete traceability across shop floors. With our integrated systems, you can track fabric from source to final garment packing. RFID and barcode tags are used together to enable bundle-wise or individual piece-wise tracking, electricity, water and stream consumtion.
Our Fabric & Apparel systems are being used across the industry. Get in touch to learn more.

visionSystem.title: Fabric Quality control using High Speed camera system
description: A quick overview of how we use technology to create impact in the industry.
features: Superhuman inspection speed with Micro-level accuracy
High-resolution fabric imagery for advanced defect detection
Integrated with optimization engine for improved yield
Automated fault labeling and tagging
Synchronized with cutting machines for efficient roll-making
Digital traceability and reduced reliance on manual inspection
Quick return on investment through enhanced operational efficiency
Working on Denim, Dyed & Greige Fabrics. Knits & Prints Coming Soon.

cameraIndustries.title: Camera Inspection in Other Industries
description: EVS (Edraak Vision System) provides reliable camera-based inspection for a wide range of industrial production lines. High-resolution cameras continuously scan products and surfaces to detect defects, damages, or inconsistencies in real time during production.
Used in pharma, food, electronics, and automotive, it quickly detects defects, reduces waste, and ensures consistent product quality.

subtabs.name: Greige Fabric
subtabs.content: Raw fabric coming straight from the loom is inspected for issues such as knots, slubs, holes, broken yarns, and uneven weave. Detecting defects early prevents faulty fabric from moving into dyeing, reducing rework and improving overall yield and consistency.

subtabs.name: Dyed
subtabs.content: After dyeing, fabrics are checked for uneven shades, stains, dye marks, streaks, and visible weave faults. Running at full production speed, the system highlights issues instantly so they can be corrected, ensuring uniform color quality and minimizing buyer rejections.

subtabs.name: Denim
subtabs.content: In denim production, defects like streaks, holes, broken yarns, slubs, neps, and barre marks are identified during the process. This continuous monitoring improves first-quality output, reduces rejection rates, and helps maintain strict buyer expectations in fast-moving production lines.

subtabs.name: On-Loom
subtabs.content: Quality is monitored directly during the weaving process to detect defects at the earliest stage. Real-time alerts allow operators to take immediate action, helping reduce downstream losses, rework, and unnecessary machine downtime.

subtabs.name: Apparel Inspection
subtabs.content: Finished garments are checked for visual defects, stitching issues, shade mismatches, and labeling errors before dispatch. This ensures every piece meets quality standards and supports faster, more reliable final inspections, reducing the chances of defective products reaching customers.

subtabs.name: Fabric Quality Control
subtabs.content: The Fabric Quality Control system streamlines quality control by logging faults and guiding inspections, feeding data into an optimization engine that creates the best cutting plans. This reduces B-grade fabric and boosts efficiency by up to 0.4%.

subtabs.name: Cutting plan optimization
subtabs.content: By utilizing advanced algorithms and artificial intelligence, Edraak’s cutting plan optimization minimizes fabric wastage and increases efficiency. This ensures that cutting plans are executed with precision, resulting in reduced operational costs and higher quality output.

subtabs.name: Width measurement
subtabs.content: Edraak’s automatic width sensing device continuously captures fabric width data, triggering alarms when deviations occur, ensuring accurate width without production stops.

subtabs.name: Labeling system
subtabs.content: Edraak's automatic labeling system marks fabric faults during production, providing essential data for efficient panel cutting without halting operations.

subtabs.name: Production tracking
subtabs.content: Edraak’s barcode scanning system provides seamless fabric tracking from input to output, resolving fabric length discrepancies and optimizing material use. High-speed scanners and digital meters log precise lengths, monitor fabric consumption, and alert teams to real-time discrepancies. This ensures accurate costing, minimizes waste, and simplifies final product verification, aligning operations for efficiency and quality assurance.

subtabs.name: Total quality management
subtabs.content: Edraak’s Factory on Phone integrates 7-0 quality control for needlepoint precision, reducing rework, tracking missing garments, and achieving cost savings while boosting efficiency.

subtabs.name: Smart sewing system
subtabs.content: Edraak's Smart Sewing System automates planning, tracking, and wages, minimizing manual errors and enhancing productivity. The RFID scanner, integrated with the traffic light system, enhances visual management.

subtabs.name: Smart wash monitoring
subtabs.content: The Smart Wash Monitoring system tracks machine performance, including power and water usage, downtime, and OEE, ensuring maximum efficiency.

subtabs.name: Machine care pro
subtabs.content: Machine Care Pro provides real-time monitoring for industrial machinery, ensuring preventive and corrective maintenance to optimize equipment performance and reduce costs.

products.title: Camera Inspection System
products.subtitle: EVS(Edraak Vision System)
products.desc: High-speed, AI-driven fabric inspection for production lines. EVS inspects fabric at-line or on-loom and provides real-time fault detection, marking, and reporting to reduce waste and speed up quality decisions.
products.greige: EVS inspects greige (raw/unfinished) fabric straight from the loom, detecting defects such as knots, slubs, holes, broken yarns, thick/thin areas, and uneven weave in real time.
products.dyed: EVS checks dyed fabrics for issues that appear after coloring, such as uneven dyeing (patchy or cloudy shades), stains, dye marks, streaks, and weave faults visible post-dye. Running at full production speed, it flags problems instantly for marking or removal, helping maintain uniform color quality.
products.denim: EVS specializes in denim inspection, identifying streaks, holes, broken yarns, slubs, neps, barre marks, and finishing defects on the fly. In busy denim lines, it shortens manual checks and lowers rejection rates.
features: Superhuman inspection speed with micro-level accuracy
High-resolution fabric imagery for advanced defect detection
Synchronized with cutting machines for efficient roll-making
Digital traceability and reduced reliance on manual inspection

pageContent.text: In today’s high-speed textile industry, traditional Fabric Quality Control methods are no longer sufficient to ensure consistent product quality. Most mills still rely heavily on human inspectors, where decisions are made at an individual level, leading to inconsistencies and lack of standardization. As production speeds increase, the gap between inspection capability and manufacturing output continues to widen, resulting in missed defects, higher wastage, and quality risks.

Manual inspection also comes with inherent limitations. Humans can typically inspect fabric at speeds of around 15 meters per minute, which is significantly lower than modern production speeds. Additionally, expecting operators to monitor full-width fabric continuously over long shifts is unrealistic. Factors such as fatigue, skill variation, and high operator turnover further reduce reliability. In many cases, mills rely on limited sampling instead of full inspection, which means a large portion of defects go undetected.

Camera inspection systems solve these challenges by bringing automation, consistency, and scalability into Fabric Quality Control. These systems use high-speed industrial cameras installed directly on the production line to capture fabric images in real time. Unlike manual methods, camera inspection ensures 100% fabric coverage, continuously monitoring the entire width without interruption. This eliminates dependency on human attention and ensures that no section of fabric goes unchecked.

One of the key advantages of camera-based Fabric Quality Control is its ability to operate at high speeds. Modern systems can capture and process images at speeds of up to 100–120 meters per minute while maintaining detection accuracy. This aligns inspection capability with production output, allowing manufacturers to scale without compromising on quality. The system works continuously, without fatigue, delivering consistent results across shifts and operators.

Another critical benefit is real-time defect detection. Camera inspection systems analyze images instantly using vision algorithms, identifying defects as soon as they occur. This allows immediate action, reducing the chances of defects propagating further down the production line. Early detection helps minimize rework, reduce wastage, and improve overall process efficiency. Camera inspection systems also act as a centralized, data-driven quality control system. Instead of relying on scattered decisions by individual operators, all inspection data is captured, processed, and stored in a structured manner. This creates a standardized quality protocol across the factory. Manufacturers gain complete visibility into defect patterns, production performance, and quality trends, enabling better decision-making and continuous improvement.

An additional advantage is the creation of a pictorial record of fabric quality. Every meter of fabric is recorded and stored as an image, which can be accessed later for review, audits, or customer claims. This level of traceability is not possible with manual inspection systems. It ensures transparency and builds trust with customers, as manufacturers can clearly demonstrate the quality of shipped fabric. Over time, camera inspection systems continue to improve. As more data is collected, the system evolves and adapts to different fabric types, defect patterns, and production conditions. This makes it increasingly effective and tailored to specific manufacturing environments. Unlike static manual processes, camera-based systems become smarter and more efficient with usage.

Ultimately, camera inspection systems transform Fabric Quality Control from a reactive process into a proactive and intelligent system. They enable manufacturers to "see everything" on the production line, ensuring that they know exactly what they are shipping to customers. By combining high-speed imaging, real-time analysis, and data-driven insights, camera inspection becomes the foundation of modern textile quality control. It is no longer just about detecting defects—it is about building a standardized, scalable, and reliable quality control system. For manufacturers aiming to compete globally, adopting camera-based Fabric Quality Control is no longer optional; it is essential for delivering consistent quality, reducing costs, and driving operational excellence.

products.title: On Loom Inspection
products.subtitle: EVS(Edraak Vision System)
products.summary: Inline fabric inspection system for looms and warp knitting machines.
products.desc: The Edraak Loom Inspection (ELI) is a vision system designed for inline surface inspection and quality monitoring of fabric. It detects weaving defects and deviations directly after the reed. Due to its modular and compact design, the inspection system can be integrated or retrofitted onto looms, weaving machines, or warp knitting machines. Using ELI prevents long-running defects and provides immediate quality information at the source of production.
bullets: Detects weaving defects directly after the reed
Modular, compact design for retrofit or integration
Prevents long-running defects and B-quality fabrics
Supports all material widths (delivered to match your width)
Dashboard for fault mapping, export, and synchronization
features: Delivered per material width and ready after integration
Custom backend↔frontend synchronization
Fault mapping software aggregates quality across all connected ELI units
Export or sync only inspection data relevant to your organization and customers

products.title: Processing Traceability
products.subtitle: Traceability System
products.summary: Edraak Traceability System is an IoT-based production planning and tracking system designed for high-speed, paperless, and real-time traceable production.
products.desc: Module for capturing and tracing processing steps across machines and enabling paperless, real-time production tracking.
products.existingProcess: The planning is done using conventional methods without any real production feedback and analysis. The process is tracked by a 24-hour or shift production system.
products.proposedProcess:
Comprehensive software for production planning and tracking.
Easy-to-use, customizable interface to guide operation.
Real-time production tracking with precise encoders.
Machine flow selection.
One-time recipe and flow creation for every article.
Flow and recipes can be automatically assigned for repeat articles.
Process completion is tracked in real time.
Complete tracking of all the operations on each roll.
Recipe and parameter verification on the machine module to minimise human error.
Previous and next machine guidance for the operator.
bullets: QR printing and high-speed QR scanning
Operator module with guided workflows
Planning & track software with recipe management
Real-time encoder-based production tracking
Machine-to-machine flow tracking and verification
Automatic recipe assignment for repeat articles
Complete roll-level operation history and audit trail

products.title: Planning Software
products.subtitle: Production Planning & Control System (PPC)
products.summary: A central Production Planning & Control (PPC) system that automates scheduling, routes, and recipe assignment while ingesting live shop-floor data to adapt plans and reduce lead time.
products.desc: PPC coordinates orders, capacity, and materials into executable production plans. It assigns machine recipes, publishes work to operator HMIs, and monitors execution using real-time signals (encoders, machine status, inspection results). When deviations occur, PPC reschedules and alerts operators to maintain throughput and quality.
need: Integration with Edraak Mapping and inspection data
products.existingProcess: Many sites plan using spreadsheets or ad-hoc systems; changes require manual coordination, causing downtime, inaccurate priorities, and fragmented traceability.
products.proposedProcess: PPC ingests orders and capacity, generates optimized schedules, assigns routes and recipes to machines, and continuously monitors execution. It raises exceptions for quality or throughput issues and rebalances work with minimal human intervention.
products.whatHappens: Orders are loaded into PPC, which allocates capacity, creates lot routes, and dispatches tasks to machine HMIs. As production runs, PPC consumes machine signals and inspection outcomes to confirm completion, update WIP status, and trigger downstream processes like labeling, cutting, or packing.
products.whatOperatorCanDo: Operators can accept/reject assignments, request rework, adjust priorities, view lot histories, and update recipe parameters from the HMI or supervisor console.
products.whyItMatters: Automates repetitive planning tasks, reduces lead times and WIP, improves on-time delivery, and ensures every production event is linked to an order for full traceability and faster root-cause analysis.

basicFlow.name: Greige Department
basicFlow.content: Maintains fabric inventory data from ERP. Issues fabric upon request from production departments.

basicFlow.name: PPC (Production Planning & Control)
basicFlow.content: Creates production plans based on market orders, fabric availability, and urgency. Monitors real-time status of all production departments.

basicFlow.name: Production Departments (Bleaching, Dyeing, Finishing)
basicFlow.content: Receive plans from PPC, create machine routes, and manage production according to machine availability and urgency.

keyProcesses.name: Fabric Request & Issuance
keyProcesses.content: Production departments raise fabric requests to Greige. Greige can issue fabric fully, partially, or reject the request.

keyProcesses.name: Route Planning
keyProcesses.content: Production departments create machine routes for lots. Route templates can be configured and loaded instantly.

keyProcesses.name: Machine Planning & WIP
keyProcesses.content: Departments can plan and monitor lots on their machines with real-time WIP visibility.

keyProcesses.name: Parameters & Recipes
keyProcesses.content: Department-wise machine parameters and recipes with controlled access (read/write/create).

features: Automated schedule generation and optimization
Real-time execution monitoring and exception handling
Recipe management with version control
Operator HMIs with task lists and KPIs
APIs for ERP and Edraak Mapping integration
Reporting and historical traceability per lot
additionalFeatures: Complete production history and change tracking
Completed orders with full details (start/end time, parameters, recipes, and lab results)
products.userRightsSecurity: System is fully segregated by departments and designations (Manager, Supervisor, Shift In-charge). Users only see their relevant orders, screens, recipes, and parameters.
bullets: Department-wise planning and control
ERP-integrated fabric issuance
Route templates and machine routing
Real-time WIP and capacity monitoring
Parameter & recipe access controls
Comprehensive reporting and audit trails

products.title: Audit Inspection
products.subtitle: Traceability System
products.summary: Improved accuracy and speed of fault mapping by enhancing the operator interface, measurements, and precise labeling.
products.desc: A real-time fabric fault mapping and labeling solution that improves operator UX, provides accurate measurements, and automates sticker application on running fabric.
products.existingProcess: Faults are marked manually and recorded on paper, then uploaded to the system. The workflow is slow and prone to human error.
products.proposedProcess: A real-time fabric fault applicator integrated with the Edraak Mapping system, capable of applying stickers to running fabric without stopping the machine at speeds up to 150 m/min. The system supports calibration for different color tags and automatically adjusts timing based on fabric speed to ensure consistent placement.
bullets: Real-time sticker application up to 150 m/min
Automatic speed-aware calibration for consistent labeling
Digital encoder for pixel-accurate fault localisation
Operator HMI and fault-marking keyboard for fast interaction
Label dispensing system with closed-loop verification
IoT-based software integrated with Edraak Mapping to remove paper logging

products.title: Fabric Batch Making
products.subtitle: Traceability System
products.summary: Automated batch-making module that aggregates rolls, measures and tags seams with QR/label stickers, and records per-roll metadata for reliable traceability and faster dispatch.
products.desc: The Fabric Batch Making module automates roll assembly and identification for downstream processing and shipping. Using encoders, sensors and a label/printer-applicator, it captures precise roll length and quantity, applies machine-readable tags at seam points, and logs each roll to the central Edraak Mapping database. This removes manual counting and written records, improves inventory accuracy, and enables seamless traceability across departments.
need: Digital encoder and seam detection sensor
Mounting and guide hardware for roll capture
products.existingProcess: Operators currently stop or slow lines to measure and tag rolls manually or record measurements on paper/spreadsheets. This process is slow, error-prone, and causes tracking gaps between production and dispatch.
products.proposedProcess: As rolls complete, the system measures length using encoder feedback, prints or dispenses a QR/label at the seam automatically, and stores metadata (length, weight if available, operator, timestamp, batch id) to the mapping database. Operators supervise via HMI and can approve, split, or merge batches without stopping production.
products.whatHappens: Sensors and encoders detect roll completion and seam position. The controller computes the correct dispenser timing, applies the tag, verifies placement with a sensor, and records the event with associated metadata for traceability.
products.whatOperatorCanDo: Configure label templates, change label rolls quickly, split or merge batches, review placement logs, reprint labels if needed, and perform maintenance without halting production.
products.whyItMatters: Eliminates manual transcription and labeling errors, reduces labor and downtime, improves inventory accuracy and traceability, and accelerates downstream operations like cutting, packing and shipping.
features: Encoder-synchronized roll length measurement
Inline QR/label printing and high-speed application
Placement verification sensor with retry/logging
Configurable batch rules and HMI-driven templates
Automatic logging to Edraak Mapping for per-roll traceability
Quick-change label spindles and low-maintenance design
bullets: Automates roll measurement and tagging
Reduces manual labor and human errors
Provides per-roll metadata for traceability and analytics
Supports quick label/media changes and multiple label sizes
Integrates with inspection systems and production workflows

products.title: Fabric Production Processing
products.subtitle: Traceability System
products.summary: An inline production processing module that enforces recipes, captures machine parameters, and records process completion with encoder and QR evidence for end-to-end traceability.
products.desc: This module records finishing and chemical treatment steps, verifies machine recipes and parameters before production runs, and logs completion events in real time. It integrates encoder feedback, QR scans and optional color sensors to ensure every roll carries an auditable process history.
need: Encoder integration for accurate length and timing
HMI for operator verification and overrides
Integration with Edraak Mapping database and analytics
products.existingProcess: Operators rely on manual checks, verbal confirmations and paper logs to verify recipes and record finishes. This causes missed parameter changes and late detection of shade or recipe errors.
products.proposedProcess: Before production starts, the system loads the recipe and parameters for the lot. Machine sensors and encoders validate runtime parameters. QR scans or encoder ticks confirm each operation step and update the central database automatically.
products.whatHappens: When a lot enters a process step, the module reads the lot ID (QR/NFC), loads the assigned recipe, and displays required parameters on the operator HMI. Encoders and sensors confirm machine speed and applied settings; successful completion is timestamped and stored together with param snapshots.
products.whatOperatorCanDo: Verify or select recipes from the HMI, acknowledge parameter checks, record exceptions or parameter changes, add notes for the lot, and trigger rework workflows if required.
products.whyItMatters: Prevents incorrect recipe application, reduces rework and rejects, and shortens the time to detect shade or parameter drift. The result is higher first-pass yield, auditable process records, and faster corrective actions.
features: Recipe enforcement with parameter snapshots
Encoder-synced operation completion logging
QR-based lot recognition and audit trail
Optional color/shade sensors for inline QC
Operator HMI with exception workflows
APIs for analytics and downstream integration
bullets: Real-time recipe and parameter verification
Automatic operation completion logging via encoders
QR/label evidence tied to each process step
Inline shade monitoring and alerts
Operator-driven exception handling and rework triggers

products.title: Highspeed Barcode + Seam Sensor
products.subtitle: Traceability System
products.summary: High-speed inline system combining optical seam detection and barcode/QR capture with encoder synchronization and AI-powered read validation for precise roll metering and automated traceability.
products.desc: An integrated sensor-fusion module that pairs an optical seam detector with a high-speed barcode/QR reader and encoder input. Frames and sensor ticks are analyzed at the edge using lightweight AI models to verify reads, flag ambiguous captures, and score seam quality. Verified seam+barcode events are recorded as position-anchored traceability records and can trigger automated labeling, batching, or downstream verification workflows.
need: Industrial high-speed barcode/QR reader (camera or laser)
Optical seam detection sensor (line-scan or camera)
Controller/HMI for operator interaction and label applicator integration
products.existingProcess: Seam detection and barcode reads are frequently handled by separate devices or manual checks. Timing drift and unsynchronized reads cause reconciliation work, missed tags, and occasional loss of roll metadata.
products.proposedProcess: The module performs low-latency sensor fusion: the seam sensor triggers a camera capture while the barcode reader attempts a read. An AI verifier analyzes the image and read confidence, and encoder ticks anchor the event position. On low-confidence reads the system retries, captures a supporting image for review, or requests HMI confirmation before committing the record.
products.whatHappens: When fabric passes, the seam sensor emits a trigger that timestamps with encoder ticks. The barcode reader and camera capture the nearby frame; the edge AI validates the barcode and assigns a confidence score. High-confidence events are logged and associated with the roll ID; ambiguous events are retried or queued for operator review. Successful events can trigger label application and update batch records in Edraak Mapping.
products.whatOperatorCanDo: Inspect recent seam+barcode events and confidence scores on the HMI, accept or reject ambiguous events, tune retry thresholds, force a label reprint, and export event logs or images for audits.
products.whyItMatters: Guarantees seam-barcode pairing with position accuracy, eliminating manual reconciliation and preventing downstream mis-labeling. This enables automated batching, accurate cutting/packing handoffs, and auditable traceability for quality and compliance.
features: Sensor fusion: seam trigger + barcode capture + encoder anchoring
AI-based read validation with confidence scoring
Retry-and-verify flow with optional image capture
Edge-first processing for low-latency decisions
Automatic label application and batch handoff integration
bullets: Inline seam detection tightly synchronized with barcode reads
High-speed barcode/QR capture with AI validation
Encoder-anchored event records for precise metering
Automatic retry and reprint on ambiguous reads
Reduces manual reconciliation and improves downstream automation

products.title: Manual Inspection
products.subtitle: Traceability System
products.summary: An on-machine inspection module that combines a fault-marking keyboard, sensors and HMI to capture precise fault locations and fabric measurements directly to the traceability database.
products.desc: Manual Inspection replaces paper-based fault logging with a machine-mounted keyboard and digital sensors. Operators mark faults and measurements on the running fabric; the system records coordinates, timestamps and optional images to provide accurate, searchable QC records.
need: Digital encoder and length measurement sensors
Network connectivity to Edraak Mapping database
products.existingProcess: Operators note faults on paper or spreadsheets, later transcribing them into the system. Measurements are taken manually and combined with visual notes, causing delays and errors.
products.proposedProcess: Operators press keys on a dedicated keyboard when a fault is observed. The system records the encoder position, fabric length and fault type instantly, optionally capturing an image. Data syncs to the central database in real time.
products.whatHappens: When a fault is marked, the encoder provides the precise location, the keyboard captures the fault code and the HMI shows a confirmation. The system timestamps and links the record to the lot ID, making the fault searchable and actionable.
products.whatOperatorCanDo: Mark fault types, add notes, request re-inspection, view previous fault history for the lot, and start a corrective workflow from the HMI.
products.whyItMatters: Eliminates double-entry and transcription errors, shortens QC cycles, and provides precise location data for analysis and corrective action—reducing scrap and rework costs.
features: Fast, ergonomic machine-mounted keyboard for fault entry
Encoder-linked location logging
Inline length and measurement capture
Optional image capture for visual evidence
Realtime sync to Edraak Mapping and analytics
bullets: Replaces paper logging with instant digital records
Gives pixel-accurate fault locations using encoder data
Supports immediate corrective workflows from the HMI
Integrates with labeling and batch systems for traceability
Improves accuracy and reduces time-to-action for QC

products.title: Weft Straightener
products.subtitle: Accessories
products.summary: The Weft Straightener determines and analyzes distortion, then corrects measured skew and bow.
products.existingProcess: Manually operated weft straighteners are in use.
products.proposedProcess: Major functional sets: Detection (Sensors/Cameras), Analyzing (Software/Control Circuit), Straightening (Rollers). Detection creates an image or signal of the weft line angle continuously. Analyzing uses a digital signal processor and software to calculate angle and generate roller commands. Straightening drives the skew and bow rollers opposite to the measured distortion to produce a straight weft line.

majorFunctionalSets.detection: Use optoelectronic sensors or imaging cameras to capture the weft line angle instantly. Sensors are optimized per fabric type.
majorFunctionalSets.analyzing: Software and a DSP analyze weft angles in real time and compute corrective commands for rollers.
majorFunctionalSets.straightening: Roller drives receive commands to move opposite the measured angle, correcting skew and bow distortions.

detectionDetails: Imaging cameras for high-resolution weft line capture
products.analyzingDetails: Algorithms compute weft line angle and issue closed-loop commands; DSP-based calculation ensures low latency and reliable control.
products.straighteningDetails: Skew and bow rollers are actuated by the controller to counter the measured distortion and maintain alignment.
features: Real-time weft angle detection
DSP-based low-latency angle computation
Closed-loop roller control for skew and bow
Supports multiple fabric types via sensor tuning
Integrates with factory PLC/SCADA and reporting
products.desc: Automated weft straightening system that detects distortion and applies precise roller corrections to maintain fabric alignment before inspection or further processing.
bullets: Detection: sensors or cameras capture weft angle continuously
Analysis: DSP/software computes corrective commands
Correction: Roller drives apply skew and bow adjustments
Reduces manual intervention and production waste
Improves downstream inspection and cutting accuracy

products.title: Labelling System
products.subtitle: Accessories
products.summary: Automated inline label applicator for fault marking, seams, and roll identification — works without stopping the production line.
products.desc: The Labelling System is an inline, high‑precision label and sticker applicator designed to automate fault marking and seam/roll identification on fabric production lines. Tightly integrated with Edraak Mapping and inspection systems, it synchronizes with encoder and vision signals to apply labels at exact coordinates while the material is running — removing manual labeling steps, reducing errors, and improving throughput and traceability.
need: Label applicator head with precision actuator
Digital encoder or seam/defect sensor integration
PLC/controller and HMI for templates and monitoring
Mounting hardware for conveyor or machine frame
Integration with Edraak Mapping / inspection outputs
products.existingProcess: Labeling and fault marking are often done manually or by stopping the line to apply stickers, which increases labor, creates inconsistencies, and reduces throughput.
products.proposedProcess: Labels are applied automatically at configured offsets or at fault coordinates reported by the inspection/encoder system. The applicator compensates for fabric speed and stretch, verifies placement with a sensor, and logs each event to the mapping database for downstream traceability.
products.whatHappens: When a defect or seam is detected, the controller computes the exact timing using encoder feedback and triggers the applicator. The dispenser places the label, a verification sensor confirms correct placement, and the system records the placement with a timestamp and location for traceability and reporting.
products.whatOperatorCanDo: Operators configure placement rules and label templates from the HMI, change label rolls quickly, view placement logs, pause or override labeling for maintenance, and adjust offsets without stopping production.
products.whyItMatters: Automates repetitive manual labeling tasks, eliminates missed or mis‑placed labels, reduces labor costs, prevents unnecessary stoppages, and provides seam- and defect-level traceability for faster root-cause analysis and higher productivity.
features: Inline label application at production speeds (model-dependent)
Encoder-synchronized dispensing for pixel-accurate placement
Closed-loop verification sensor to confirm placement
Support for multiple label sizes/types and quick-change spindles
Configurable templates, rules, and HMI-driven setup
Integration with Edraak Mapping and inspection data for full traceability
bullets: Automates label application and fault marking
Reduces manual labor and line stoppages
Ensures consistent label placement and verification
Provides seam- and defect-level traceability
Quick maintenance and label roll replacement

products.title: Width Measurement System
products.subtitle: Accessories
products.summary: To measure the width of the fabric in real-time.
products.existingProcess: Checked manually at random intervals, causing a slow production and a high probability of error.
products.proposedProcess: Edraak’s automatic width-sensing device continuously captures fabric width data, triggering alarms when deviations occur, ensuring accurate width without production stops.
products.desc: Edraak’s automatic width-sensing device continuously captures fabric width data, triggering alarms when deviations occur, ensuring accurate width without production stops.
bullets: Real-time width capture
Automatic alarms on deviation
No production stops required
Dashboard & software interface for trend analysis
Configurable thresholds and alerts

products.title: In-Line Moisture Meter
products.subtitle: Accessories
products.summary: Senses the moisture of fabric on the production line.
products.existingProcess: Manual inspection by the checker; machine speed is adjusted manually by the operator.
products.proposedProcess: The fabric moisture will be tested at three points along the width, and machine speed will be varied based on the analysis. Electrical resistance between two poles of an electrode is measured; configurations include an electrode with a counter-roller, two isolated rollers, or other electrode arrangements. Measurements are suitable behind dryers, stenters, sizing machines, cylinder dryers, humidifiers, or in front of padding mangles. Measurement and control of moisture improve process stability, product quality, and energy efficiency.
pros: Optimizes process quality, efficiency, and reproducibility
Reduces energy consumption through better dryer control
Maintains high and consistent output moisture to avoid over-drying/overdying
Enables automated feedback control and reporting

sensorTypes.notes: Wheel sensors provide non-invasive measurement; measuring range and accuracy depend on fabric and sensor setup.
Suitable for natural fibers or blends with not more than 5% synthetics; may require electrostatic precautions.

limitations: Wheel-type measuring range: ~4 .. 16% for cotton; accuracy lower than roller methods
Roller-type recommended for natural fibers and low-synthetic blends; limited protection against electrostatics
products.desc: Inline moisture meter for continuous fabric moisture monitoring and automated control.
bullets: Three-point width sensing for better cross-width control
Automatic feedback to machine-speed control
Configurable electrode arrangements (roller, wheel)
Dashboard, reporting and feedback outputs for process control
Helps avoid over-drying and improves product reproducibility

products.title: Spectrophotometer
products.subtitle: Accessories
products.summary: Senses the color shade of fabric on the production line.
products.existingProcess: A sample is cut, and the fabric is tested in the lab.
products.proposedProcess: The color shade of fabric will be sensed and analysed on the production line in real time. An alarm will be triggered in case of deviation. A complete report of the fabric will be prepared at the end of the process.
advantages: Multiple color indicators can be measured
Waterproof and dust-proof, protection class IP66
LED light source for longer service
Internal calibration to ensure high instrument stability
Double light path spectroscopy for high speed and precision
Improves accuracy compared to machine-vision color measurements when paired with ESM for inline monitoring
products.desc: Industrial inline spectrophotometer for continuous color monitoring and quality control.
bullets: Real-time color sensing on the production line
Automatic deviation alarms and thresholding
Edge-ready for factory integration (IP66 rated)
Dashboard integration and end-of-run reporting
Internal self-calibration and long-life LED illumination

productsHero.subtitle: Our Products
productsHero.title: Products
productsHero.description: High-speed textile inspection hardware and industrial systems to reduce waste, increase yield, and ensure consistent quality across production lines.

technology.title: Technology Domains
technology.description: A quick overview of how we use technology to create impact in the industry.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence
items.description: Cut plan optimization leverages AI to analyze fabric quality, defects, and order constraints, generating efficient layouts that minimize wastage, improve yield, and ensure consistent decision-making across production processes.

items.title: Computer Vision
items.description: Our computer vision platform learns fabric patterns and detects defects in real time, enabling automated inspection, high-speed monitoring, and consistent quality control without reliance on manual human inspection.

items.title: Robotics
items.description: Robotic systems enable precise marking and removal of defective areas, reducing manual intervention, improving accuracy, and ensuring seamless integration with inspection and cutting processes for higher operational efficiency.

items.title: Spectrophotometer
items.description: Spectrophotometer-based monitoring detects subtle color variations during production, ensuring consistency across batches, reducing rejections, and minimizing line losses caused by shade mismatches or dyeing inconsistencies.

items.title: RFID & Barcoding
items.description: RFID and barcoding systems track production in real time, enabling precise material identification, process visibility, and root cause analysis by linking defects, operators, and machines across the manufacturing workflow.

items.title: Energy Monitoring
items.description: Energy monitoring systems capture real-time consumption data across machines, enabling accurate costing, identifying inefficiencies, and supporting data-driven decisions to optimize energy usage and reduce operational expenses.

items.title: Data & Business Analytics
items.description: Gathering data from various sources, including existing and proprietary ones, to develop a more powerful decision-making engine that turns inspection, production, and energy data into actionable insights.

items.title: Visual Fault Detection
items.description: Ultra highspeed camera inspection system for Woven and Non-Woven fabrics.

items.title: Fabric Color Sensors
items.description: Spectrometers, installed on your machines helps you identify color and shade variation issues at the production stage.

items.title: Sewing Traceability & QC
items.description: Bundle Flow or Single piece, we got you covered. Equipped with latest IoT device installed on each machines. We can log production & quality data.

items.title: Fabric Width Sensing
items.description: Use Highspeed cameras to detect fabric width with pin point accuracy and control parameters of your machine in real-time.

items.title: Fabric Traceability
items.description: linked with digital encoders, Ultra High Speed Scanners detect seam optically, also the Barcode in real-time. Giving you the most accurate fabric meters for departmental transfer. Energy Consumption data can also be linked.

items.title: Digital Data Loggers
items.description: Industrial grade digital data loggers to allow users to input data manually and Also link machine & sensors.

items.title: Energy Monitoring
items.description: Real-time energy monitoring system that tracks exact usage of electricity, water, steam, and air per unit produced (piece, roll, batch, or product).

items.title: Automated Production Tracking and Data Logging
items.excerpt: Automated production management by replacing manual data entry, improving accuracy and reducing operational costs

items.title: AI in Cutting Plan Optimization
items.excerpt: A new fabric cutting algorithm reduced wastage, cut costs, and improved real-time traceability, providing a more efficient and paperless workflow.

items.title: Realtime Process Quality Control
items.excerpt: A new solution automated with measurement, speeding up quality control, improving accuracy, and enabling real-time, paperless operations.

items.title: Quality Assurance via Business Intelligence
items.excerpt: Real-time analytics, streamlined decision-making, and improved efficiency by providing easy-to-use, data-driven insights.

careers.title: Careers
careers.description: Join the team that’s building for the future, Check available openings.

items.title: Senior Computer Vision Engineer
items.short: Design and deploy computer vision models for high-speed fabric inspection.
items.description: We are looking for a Senior Computer Vision Engineer to lead our EVS model development, optimize inference pipelines, and integrate algorithms with the production system.
responsibilities: Develop and optimize real-time vision models
Work with engineering to deploy models on edge hardware
Lead labeling and dataset curation efforts
qualifications: MS/PhD in Computer Vision or related field
3+ years experience in production CV systems

items.title: Business Development Manager
items.short: Grow partnerships and close deals in textile and industrial sectors.
items.description: Lead go-to-market efforts, manage key accounts, and work with product teams to align solutions to customer needs.
responsibilities: Identify and pursue new business opportunities
Maintain client relationships and track pipeline
qualifications: 5+ years in B2B sales or BD roles
Experience in industrial or textile sectors preferred
Proven track record of closing enterprise deals

items.title: Frontend Engineer
items.short: Build elegant UI and client-side features for our web apps.
items.description: Work on React/Next.js applications, implement designs, and collaborate with designers and backend engineers.

items.title: Backend Engineer
items.short: Design and maintain APIs and data services.
items.description: Build scalable backend services, REST/GraphQL APIs, and data processing pipelines.

items.title: QA Engineer
items.short: Maintain product quality through testing and automation.
items.description: Develop automated test suites, manage QA processes, and work closely with engineers to prevent regressions.

items.title: Product Manager
items.short: Define product roadmap and coordinate cross-functional teams.
items.description: Lead product discovery, prioritize features, and work with stakeholders to deliver value.

items.title: DevOps Engineer
items.description: Automate deployments, monitor systems, and improve reliability and scalability.

company.about: Edraak Systems is a leader in AI-powered quality control and traceability solutions for textile, apparel, and manufacturing industries.
